steps.reveal: Omnia = all things (acc. pl. neut., object of 'vincit')

hints.nominative: While 'omnia' could be nom. pl. neut., here it is accusative, object of 'vincit'
hints.ablative: Ablative plural would be 'omnibus' — 'omnia' is accusative
hints.genitive: Genitive plural would be 'omnium' — 'omnia' is accusative

hints.to bind: That would be 'vincire' — 'vincit' comes from 'vincere' (to conquer)
hints.to live: That would be 'vivere' — 'vincit' comes from 'vincere' (to conquer)
hints.to avenge: That would be 'vindicare' — 'vincit' comes from 'vincere' (to conquer)

hints.perfect indicative: Perfect would be 'vicit' — 'vincit' with -i- is present indicative (3rd conj.)
hints.present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'vincat' — 'vincit' is present indicative
hints.future indicative: Future would be 'vincet' — 'vincit' is present indicative

hints.vincio, vincire, vinxi, vinctum: These are the parts of 'vincire' (to bind)
hints.vivo, vivere, vixi, victum: These are the parts of 'vivere' (to live)
hints.video, videre, vidi, visum: These are the parts of 'videre' (to see)

hints.accusative: Accusative would be 'Amorem' — 'Amor' is nominative, the subject
hints.vocative: While vocative of 3rd decl. looks like nom., 'Amor' is the subject of 'vincit' here

hints.accusative: While 'nos' can be accusative, here it is nominative, subject of 'cedamus'
hints.ablative: Ablative would be 'nobis' — 'nos' is nominative here
hints.dative: Dative would be 'nobis' — 'nos' is nominative, subject of 'cedamus'

hints.to cut: That would be 'caedere' — 'cedamus' comes from 'cedere' (to yield/give way)
hints.to fall: That would be 'cadere' — 'cedamus' comes from 'cedere' (to yield)
hints.to sing: That would be 'canere' — 'cedamus' comes from 'cedere' (to yield)

hints.present indicative: Present indicative would be 'cedimus' — 'cedamus' with -a- is subjunctive (3rd conj.)
hints.future indicative: Future would be 'cedemus' — 'cedamus' is present subjunctive (hortatory)
hints.imperfect subjunctive: Imperfect subjunctive would be 'cederemus' — 'cedamus' is present subjunctive

hints.caedo, caedere, cecidi, caesum: These are the parts of 'caedere' (to cut/kill)
hints.cado, cadere, cecidi, casum: These are the parts of 'cadere' (to fall)
hints.cano, canere, cecini, cantum: These are the parts of 'canere' (to sing)

steps.reveal: Amori = to Love (dat. sg., 'cedere' takes dative)

hints.nominative: Nominative would be 'Amor' — 'Amori' with -i is dative singular
hints.ablative: Ablative would be 'Amore' — 'Amori' is dative, required by 'cedere'

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'amoris' ends in -is (3rd declension)
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'amoris' ends in -is
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -ūs — 'amoris' ends in -is

translation: Love conquers all; let us too yield to Love
